tiny_practice.action: Take a deep breath and silently acknowledge moments when speaking truth feels costly

two_step.reflect_question: How do you respond when speaking God's truth leads to personal opposition?

options.text: Stand firm with love and grace

creative_prompt.instruction: Draw a picture representing a time when you felt pressured to stay silent about something important

reevaluation.question: How has this chapter challenged your understanding of prophetic ministry?
options: Speaking truth can be personally painful
Faithfulness matters more than personal comfort

three_choice.question: Which aspect of prophetic suffering resonates most deeply?

choices.expansion: Jeremiah's raw vulnerability reveals the profound psychological toll of speaking uncomfortable truths.
The prophet's conflict with Pashhur symbolizes institutional resistance to transformative spiritual messages.

choices.text: Inner tension between despair and divine calling
choices.expansion: Jeremiah oscillates between wanting to be silent and feeling compelled to speak God's word.

socratic_nudge.question: When does speaking truth become an act of personal sacrifice, and at what point does silence become complicity?

moral_slider.question: How do personal emotional struggles intersect with spiritual responsibility?

reevaluation.question: How might this text challenge your understanding of prophetic ministry?
options: Prophets are not immune to human emotions
Spiritual calling involves significant personal cost
Institutional resistance is an expected response to transformative messages

thematic_summaries.title: Prophetic Persecution
thematic_summaries.summary: Jeremiah experiences direct persecution from religious leadership for delivering God's uncomfortable message. His confrontation with Pashhur demonstrates the profound personal cost of prophetic ministry and unwavering divine communication.

thematic_summaries.title: Divine Calling and Inner Struggle
thematic_summaries.summary: Despite experiencing intense personal suffering and emotional turmoil, Jeremiah remains committed to his prophetic calling. His raw emotional dialogue with God reveals the profound internal conflict between personal comfort and divine mandate.

reevaluation.question: How does Jeremiah's response to persecution reflect authentic spiritual resilience?
options: Demonstrates unwavering commitment despite personal cost
Reveals human vulnerability within divine calling
Illustrates transformative power of prophetic suffering
Highlights theological tension between divine sovereignty and human experience
